locations.aboutUs: The Potter's House Church has been open to feed anyone who is in need for quite some time now. We have always put forth a grateful, giving heart to this community and we strive to serve you every week.
locations.notes: When you come to our distribution, we have a moment of pre-registration before our drive thru style service comes into play. Please walk up to the waiting area for registration to begin. We will get you registered and hand you a number that you will return to us as you drive up. Ask any of our volunteers for guidance at any point to better assist you.

locations.aboutUs:
Society of St. Vincent de Paul helps people with overdue bills for rent and some utilities.

For PAST-DUE rents, gas or electricity, call 916-485-3482 FIRST to confirm eligibility for assistance from 9 - 11AM on a Tuesday or Thursday.  Do NOT just drop in.

Also, you must bring actual, hard-copy paper bill - a photo will NOT do - with you when you visit us.

 

We also provide food assistance.
locations.notes: IN CASE OF RAIN, we are closed.

Sacramento Food Bank & Family Services (SFBFS) and the Health Education Council (HEC) in partnership with the ReIMAGINE Mack Road Foundation and Mack Road Partnership will distribute food boxes and non-perishable items bi-weekly.

When: 3:30 pm-5:30 pm

How Often: Every 1st & 3rd Wednesdays of the month

Where: Mack Road Valley Hi Community Center7833 Center Pkwy, Sacramento, CA 95823

If you have questions, please contact Health Education Council at (916) 556-3344. For more information, please visit our website at healthedcouncil.org

locations.notes:
Temporary Car Entrance to Carmichael Food Closet STARTING March 30 – August 1, 2026

The Carmichael Food Closet will be OPEN during the church’s parking lot construction; however, GUESTS IN CARS will enter from FAIR OAKS BLVD heading southwest next to Sana Market and Furniture Store. Drive past the furniture store and to the right of the former 99Cent Store building to the cyclone fence. The gate will open at 1:00 pm for daytime pick-up or 5:30 pm on Thursday evening. Please DO NOT arrive before then.

The process for WALK-UP GUESTS will remain the same and our hours of operation will remain the same.
February 14, 2026 - free health screenings available for all

locations.aboutUs:
Homeless bag-to-go: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday and Friday 10:30am-11am

Clients arriving after this time will be asked to wait until we have a break in registering clients in cars.

On-Foot Full Distribution: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday and Friday 11:30am-1pm (age 60+)

12:30pm-1pm (under age 60)

(AVAILABLE TO CLIENTS WITHOUT TRANSPORTATION ARRIVING ON FOOT. ALL OTHERS ARE SUBJECT TO LONGER WAIT TIMES OR MAY BE TURNED AWAY)

Drive-through

M, Tu, Th and Fri

The first hour (11:00 a.m. - 12:00 p.m.) of distribution is reserved for our guests who are 60 years of age and older, and noon (12:00 p.m.-1:00 p.m.) for anyone under 60. Our distribution ends at 1p.m.. Registration closes when capacity is reached. Food distributions are held via a drive-through. Oversized vehicles cannot be accommodated at the drive-through but are required to wait in the distribution line to register before parking and walking up to receive food.

 

Wednesday-CLOSED
locations.notes:
CLICK FOR CLOSED DAYS

 

NO IDLE ZONE

While you wait, you will be asked to turn off your vehicle to protect the health and safety of our clients and volunteers.  We follow the State of California guidelines regarding vehicle idling as part of this organization's policy. Please refer to this website for additional information.

 

Breathe easier, California! Turn off your engine.

 

!!!HIGH HEAT WARNING!!!

In the event temperatures reach 90 and above, our distribution is subject to closure without notice and/or receiving a distribution.

locations.notes: Typically closes at 12pm on Food Distribution Tuesdays OR until produce is no longer available. First come First served.

Serving Orangevale, Fair Oaks, Folsom & Citrus Heights only.

We are a drive-thru distribution. No Parking on Central Ave at all, please return 15 minutes later to not block the road.

Line starts 30 minutes before distribution, we will ask you to leave if you have arrived early. Before we open, no waiting on Main Ave or Central, please wait in the parking lot on Greenback by the Carl's Jr. 

 

WEDNESDAY DISTRIBUTION: Reserved for our working families.  We ask that you choose a daytime distribution whenever possible.

locations.locationName: St. Mark Lutheran Twice Blessed Food Pantry
locations.foodServiceTypes: Curbside or Drive Through, Walking Through

locations.aboutUs:
Anyone in need of food is free to come visit us weekly. No appointments are necessary. First time guests can just come on in, fill out our simple registration, and you’ll be good to go. Drive through and walk-in service available. Our team is here to help ensure that you and your family have access to nourishing food, no matter what. So, whether you need a helping hand for a short time or something more long-term, we’re here for you. There is wheelchair access and a bathroom. Please be respectful of other participants. Please remember to bring your bags. 

Hours and location: Food distribution: every Thursday* 3:15 PM – 5:00 PM. Essentials distributions: every third Thursday of the month, 3:15 PM – 4:30 PM. Location: in the parking lot at Christ Church United Methodist, 1717 Yulupa Avenue, Santa Rosa, CA. Pantry guests should bring their Pantry ID cards. Please return bags for reuse. What we save on replacing bags we can use to buy more food.?*We do close for most national holidays that fall on a Thursday.

locations.locationName: Community Baptist Church- 4 Seasons of Giving
locations.aboutUs: Serving, feeding and helping those in need within our community to the best of our ability and with what we have available, while creating a safe, encouraging, supportive environment and treating people with respect and dignity- regardless of race, gender, religious affiliation, status, etc.. Everyone is welcome. We strive to give everyone a positive and supportive experience, as well as giving them the assistance that will help with food and/or diapers. We will not tolerate hate speech, harassment, racism, or any disrespectful behavior that causes an unsafe or hostile environment for our staff or those coming to get assistance. Thank you.
locations.notes: We now have boxes available for our unhoused community, that are being given out on the same days/times as our scheduled distributions. If you need one outside of those days/times, please call or text Maria (707)890-1210 to schedule that with the church.

locations.locationName: CPI, Family Resource Center at John Reed Elementary
locations.aboutUs:
The Family Resource Center is here to connect parents with essential support services tailored to meet your family’s unique needs. Our dedicated Community Health Workers collaborate one-on-one with families to identify their specific requirements and provide personalized assistance. Services Offered at the Family Resource Center Include:

Parenting Support:

Individual Parent Education Courses Parenting Classes Support Groups Specialized Trainings

Basic Needs Assistance:

Food Resources Diaper Support Clothing Referrals Counseling Referrals Legal Service Referrals PG&E Bill Assistance Support with Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) & Relief for Energy Assistance

Financial & Career Resources:

Employment & Career Training Medi-Cal / CalFresh Application Support4C’s Child Care Application

Support Health & Wellness:

Health Resources School Advocacy Youth Programs Domestic Violence Resources Foster Youth / Parent Support Perinatal & Postpartum Services Substance Abuse Resources Veteran Resources

Additional Support:

Recreational Activities Immigration Resources Resource Navigation Assistance

Please contact us to learn more about our services and discover how we can help connect you with the resources you need.
locations.notes:
Reminder: The John Reed Family Resource Center is limited exclusively to Rohnert Park residents. If you plan to visit John Reed Family Resource Center, you MUST sign in at the school's main office. To be eligible for services, you must be the parent or caregiver of a child, aged 0 to 18, living in your household. Individual youth under 18 are also eligible for services.

Thank you,

-Family Resource Center Staff

locations.notes: The Living Room Life Center is available for women and children only. The Life Center is designed to be the welcoming entry point for their services.

locations.locationName: GAP Food Ministry of the WELL COMMUNITY FELLOWSHIP
locations.aboutUs:
GAP Food Ministry:

The GAP Ministry team recognizes that God has called each of us to be a blessing to the people in Stanislaus County, who struggle with food insecurity. GAP Food Ministry serves as the hands & feet of Christ to provide good, quality foods to the hungry. God has blessed GAP volunteers with love, compassion & the desire to reach out to those in need. SGAP groceries are shared with love & respect to those who come for assistance, it is given freely without judgement or condemnation. It is our intention that our SGAP, TEFAP, EFB & FIGS clients receive not only groceries for their physical hunger, but also kindness & grace to feed their spiritual and emotional hunger.

"For I was hungry and you fed me, I was thirsty and you gave me water, I was a stranger and you invited me into your home."

                                                                                                                                                                                   Matthew 25:35

locations.locationName: Extended Child Care Coalition of Sonoma County Inc

locations.aboutUs:
Monday through Friday. Sit down lunches begin promptly at 11:30 am. We serve the items as follows. No take-out meals (grab & go).

Each meal course ends when the next one begins. Please be prompt.

11:30 am Soup

11:45 am Salad

12:00 pm Main Meal

We also have Meals-On-Wheels for seniors (60+) who are physically or mentally disabled and unable to shop and/or prepare their own meals. We service seniors in the Clearlake Oaks and Glenhaven zip codes.
locations.notes: Program eligibility: Seniors 60 + only

locations.aboutUs: Park in the lot on the west side of our building or on the street.  Visitors/clients will access the main church building AND the food distribution areas from the parking lot.  The KUMON lot is off limits, please.
locations.notes:
Our church admin hours vary throughout the week. 

Donations of non-perishable food or clean, gently used clothes may be left at any time. Please check signage on the porch for the specific drop-off location.

locations.foodOfferings: Dairy, Diapers, Eggs, Fruits & Vegetables, Meat, Prepared Food / Grab and Go, Shelf Stable/Non-Perishable Goods

locations.aboutUs:
We are a farm-based animal assisted and horticultural therapy program for children with histories of abuse and neglect.

We serve youth 3 - 18 years of age in our group therapy program and youth 14 -18 years of age in our one-on-one mentoring work readiness program.

There are over 50 permanent resident animals in our program - many with histories similar to the children we serve.  Once rescued and brought to Forget Me Not Farm the animals will live the remainder of their lives here and will be cared for by loving staff and child participants. http://www.forgetmenotfarm.org

locations.notes: We always have enough food whether you arrive at 1 PM or 3:00 - we serve over 300 households every Friday. We are located in downtown Windsor with a tiny parking lot, there is plenty of free parking across the street at the new SMART Train lot.. If you arrive early you will be told to come back after 3. Always check our website for holiday closures. https://wsapantry.org

locations.aboutUs: Our Mission: We serve as stewards that provide USDA food through TEFAP (The Emergency Food Assistance Program), as well as in the collection of surplus and donated food, ensuring its effective distribution, free of charge to those facing food insecurities in Lake County. Clear Lake Gleaners has eight (8) locations that dispense food commodities around the Lake twice per month.

locations.aboutUs:
Food distribution is held inside St. Hubert's Hall, behind the Guerneville Fire Station. Dog and cat food is provided by the Humane Society on the 4th Saturday, usually outdoors in the parking area.

Please bring your own shopping bags and/or boxes whenever possible.

Line numbers will be handed out starting at 8am.
